idea-plugin.name: Docker Compose Postgres Datasources
idea-plugin.description:
Turns the PostgreSQL services in your docker-compose files into ready-to-use database connections.

On project open — and on demand via Tools | Sync Docker Compose Datasources — the plugin scans docker-compose*.yml / compose*.yml for postgres / postgis services and registers each as a PostgreSQL data source pointing at the published host port, with the user, database and password filled in so you can connect in one click.

  • Reads POSTGRES_USER, POSTGRES_DB, POSTGRES_PASSWORD and the host port published for container port 5432 (short and long ports syntax).
  • Reconciles on every sync: adds new services, updates changed ones, and removes services that were deleted from compose.
  • Only ever touches data sources it created — your own connections are never modified.
  • Skips build output, node_modules, cdk.out and other IDE-excluded or VCS-ignored locations.

Requires IntelliJ IDEA Ultimate (uses the bundled Database Tools).

action.description: Scan docker-compose files for PostgreSQL services and register them as data sources
